latest.content:
9 Jul 2026 11:00
RNS Number : 7107L
Reach PLC
09 July 2026
9 July 2026
Reach plc ("the Company")
Notice of Results
Reach plc will announce its interim financial results for the 6 months ended 30 June 2026 on Wednesday, 22 July 2026.
Results presentation and webcast arrangements
Piers North, Chief Executive Officer, and Darren Fisher, Chief Financial Officer, will host a presentation for investors and analysts at 9:00am (UK) on Wednesday, 22 July 2026 at Norton Rose Fulbright LLP's offices, 3 More London Riverside, London SE1 2AQ. If you plan to attend the results presentation in person, please email
investorrelations@reachplc.com.
A live webcast of the presentation, including Q&A, will be available via our website at
www.reachplc.com/investors
or on
https://brrmedia.news/RCH_HY26
.
This will be available for playback after the event.
Investor presentation via Investor Meet Company
Reach plc is pleased to announce that Piers North, Chief Executive Officer, and Darren Fisher, Chief Financial Officer, will provide a live presentation relating to the Half Year Results via Investor Meet Company on 27 July 2026 at 11:00am (UK).
The presentation is open to all existing and potential shareholders. Questions can be submitted prior to the event via your Investor Meet Company dashboard up until 26 July 2026 at 9:00am (UK), or at any time during the live presentation.
Investors can sign up to Investor Meet Company for free and 'add' to meet the Company via:
https://www.investormeetcompany.com/reach-plc/register-investor
Enquiries
Reach plc
Jo Britten, Director of Investor Relations
investorrelations@reachplc.com
+44 (0)7557 557447

latest.title: Purchase of Shares for the Employee Benefit Trust
latest.content:
2 Jun 2026 12:30
RNS Number : 6739G
Reach PLC
02 June 2026
2 June 2026
Reach plc
(the Company)
Purchase of Shares for the Employee Benefit Trust
The Company has been notified by the Trustee of the Reach Employee Benefit Trust (EBT) that the EBT has purchased a total of 142,493 Ordinary Shares of 10p each in the Company. Further details are set out below.
This purchase is in line with the position confirmed previously to shareholders that the Company intends to buy shares into the EBT when it is appropriate to do so to aid the management of overall dilution from share plans.
Date
Exchange
Number of shares
Price per share
(pence)
1 June 2026
XLON
142,493
0.549807
The shares will be held in the EBT, which is a discretionary trust for the benefit of the Group's employees. The shares will be used to satisfy the exercise of share options by employees or the release of restricted shares, including to Persons Discharging Managerial Responsibility (PDMRs) of the Company.
Further to this purchase, the EBT holds a total of 2,738,602 Ordinary Shares which represents 0.85% of the Company's issued share capital (322,085,269 including Treasury shares of 3,748,968). The total number of Ordinary Shares of 10p each in issue (excluding treasury shares) is 318,336,301.
The Company will continue to keep this under review and will look to make further purchases into the EBT when it is appropriate to do so.
Enquiries
Reach plc
020 7293 3000
Piers North, Chief Executive Officer
Darren Fisher, Chief Financial Officer
Teneo
Giles Kernick
020 7353 4200

latest.title: Trading Update
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 1318D
Reach PLC
06 May 2026
6 May 2026
R
each plc ("The Company") Q1 Trading update for the 3-month period to 31 March 2026
On track to deliver market expectations, despite digital headwinds
Q1 YOY %
Digital revenue1
(8.1)
-
Direct revenue
(4.5)
-
Indirect revenue
(10.5)
Print revenue
(6.6)
-
Circulation revenue
(5.5)
-
Advertising revenue
(12.8)
Group revenue
(6.9)
Trading in Q126 was affected by the ongoing disruption in search and referral volumes, in a continuation of the trends we first reported on in July 2025. On-platform referral volumes, mainly from Google, were materially lower and reduced across the quarter, resulting in a 8.1% decline in digital revenue.
These ongoing headwinds reinforce the validity of our three priorities and we continue to execute our strategy and diversify revenues. Actions include growing off-platform audiences, expanding video content and successfully launching premium subscriptions which are now live across 11 sites. Print revenue decreased 6.6%, with circulation and advertising revenues remaining reliable, supported by a cover price increase and strong promotional activity.
Outlook
While on-platform audiences are showing signs of stabilisation, we continue to be cautious on digital revenues. We remain confident in delivering the reduction in operating costs, and are on track to deliver in line with market expectations for the full year.2
Piers North,
Chief Executive
"
We are undoubtedly in the middle of yet another big shift in the media world as the digital referral landscape continues to change, but we are navigating this uncertainty appropriately. We have the benefit of our scale and a portfolio of trusted brands, reaching 35m people every month and we continue to develop new revenue streams, in particular with our growing subscriptions business."
Notes:
1
Direct revenues:
Advertising or commercial revenues that are generated from direct engagement with the advertiser, agency or consumer.
Indirect revenues
: Advertising or commercial revenues that are generated indirectly such as revenue on social platforms (off-platform) or programmatically on owned and operated websites (on-platform).
2
Market expectations
compiled by the Company are an average of analyst published forecasts - consensus adjusted operating profit for FY26 £95.9m.
3
Reach plc half year results will be reported Wednesday 22 July 2026.
Enquiries
Reach
communications@reachplc.com
Piers North, Chief Executive Officer
Darren Fisher, Chief Financial Officer
Lija Kresowaty, Director of Communications
Jo Britten, Investor Relations
+44 (0)7557 557447
Teneo
reachplc@teneo.com
Giles Kernick
+44 (0)207 353 4200
About Reach
We're Reach plc, the UK and Ireland's largest commercial news publisher. We connect with people on and offline, sharing our trusted content through 120+ brands, from household names like the Mirror, Express, Daily Record and Daily Star, to local titles like MyLondon, BelfastLive and the Manchester Evening News, plus our growing US brands like the Irish Star. Every month we reach 69% of the UK online population, 9% of the US population, and over 112m social followers around the world.

company_30s.what_they_do: Reach plc runs the biggest news‑and‑media group in the UK, publishing national newspapers, regional titles, magazines and a growing digital platform that delivers news, sport and entertainment to millions of readers every day.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company owns a huge audience base that advertisers want to reach. Its print legacy gives it a steady revenue stream, while the digital arm is the only part that can grow fast and attract new advertisers, making the business a key player in the UK media landscape.
company_30s.current_state: Reach has been trimming its print operation and tightening costs, while investing in digital content and data‑driven advertising. The group is still burning cash but has begun to show signs of stabilising, with regular earnings reports and a small share‑purchase programme that suggests some confidence from insiders.

rns_narrative: The company has been busy with routine corporate updates: quarterly results, an AGM, a director‑shareholding disclosure, and an employee‑benefit trust share purchase. These items show that management is keeping the board and shareholders informed and that insiders are buying into the business.
A second theme is the ongoing restructuring of the print division and the push toward digital monetisation. The trading update and AGM notes mention cost‑cutting targets and new digital initiatives.
The third thread is governance: a change in director composition and a share‑purchase programme that could signal confidence or a way to keep the share price from falling further.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism amid a silent market
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of commentators are hopeful that Reach’s digital pivot will start paying off, citing the steady traffic growth and the board’s commitment to cost control.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Many are worried that the print decline is irreversible, that advertising revenue is still shrinking, and that the company’s debt load could become unmanageable if digital growth stalls.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: After the AGM the chatter dipped slightly, but overall engagement remains low. The community is largely on standby, waiting for a clear positive signal before moving.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat, meaning no one is actively searching for Reach. A silent market is a perfect stage for a surprise bounce if a catalyst hits.

near_term_catalysts: The next earnings release on 9 Jul 2026 could provide a clear beat or a new digital‑growth narrative. A positive surprise in earnings or revenue would be the first sign that the market is starting to see value.
Management’s commentary on the effectiveness of the print‑cutting plan or a new partnership with a tech firm could spark buying interest.
A share‑buyback announcement or a modest dividend lift would signal that insiders are confident in the company’s future cash flow.
A volume spike that exceeds the 20‑day average by 150‑200 % would confirm that the market is moving beyond capitulation.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a 2‑week earnings beat, a 30‑day volume surge, a management statement that digital traffic is now profitable, or a share‑buyback announcement. Any of these would give a clear trigger for a mean‑reversion trade.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock sits in the early phase of a potential recovery. The crash has already hit deep levels, but capitulation looks complete because volume has thinned and sentiment is muted. Confirmation will come from a catalyst that restores confidence, after which the price can start to climb back toward the middle of its 52‑week range.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: Reach’s share price has dropped more than 80 % from its peak, which is the depth that historically delivers the biggest rebounds. If the price rebounds to the midpoint of its 52‑week range, the upside is roughly 200 % from today’s level. The mean‑reversion thesis is strong because the company has a clear restructuring plan and a large, loyal audience that can be monetised through digital advertising.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The print business is still shrinking, advertising revenue remains under pressure, and the company carries a sizeable debt load. If digital traffic fails to become profitable, further cost cuts could push the stock into a downward spiral, and a worst‑case scenario would be a further 10‑15 % drop before the company is forced to sell assets or seek a bailout.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: The maximum loss is limited to the current price, while the potential upside is at least 200 %. Even a 50 % rebound would more than double the investment. The risk‑reward ratio is therefore comfortably above 10:1, making this a very attractive asymmetrical play.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a positive earnings surprise or a share‑buyback announcement. Use a dollar‑cost‑averaging approach: buy 10‑20 % of the target allocation every 3‑5 days as the price climbs. Place a stop just below the recent low (around the 20‑day low) to protect against a brief retracement.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until you see a volume surge that is at least 150 % above the 20‑day average and a clear statement from management that digital traffic is now profitable. That would be a clean entry point.
playbook.if_passing: If the company announces a major restructuring that includes a sale of key assets, a significant debt write‑down, or a delisting notice, consider exiting. These events would signal that the company is no longer in a position to recover.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Reach has crashed deeply and sits in a silent market; a positive earnings beat or a digital‑growth announcement could trigger a mean‑reversion, offering a high‑reward, low‑downside trade.
